---
title: "Project GDP Supplemental"
embed-resources: true
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
editor: visual
---

### Load New Packages!

```{r}
#| label: Load in Data and libraries
#| results: 'hide'
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| echo: false
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(readxl)
library(janitor)
library(gt)
#install.packages("xfun")
library(xfun)
library(httr)
library(tidyjson)
library(jsonlite)
library(rvest)
library(chromote)
#install.packages("hexbin")
library(hexbin)
library(ggridges)
library(viridis)

#NEW PACKAGES TO ADD
library(stringr)
library(stringi)
#install.packages("patchwork")
library(patchwork)

cases_month <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-06-24/cases_month.csv') |>
  clean_names()
cases_year <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-06-24/cases_year.csv')
gdp_web <- readr::read_csv("gdp_web.csv")
```

### New Data Joining!

```{r}
clean_country <- function(x) {
  x |>
    stri_trans_general("Latin-ASCII") |>
    str_to_lower() |>
    str_replace_all("&", "and") |>
    str_remove_all("[[:punct:]]") |>
    str_squish()
}

gdp_web_clean <- gdp_web |>
  rename(year = scraped_year) |>
  mutate(
    year = as.numeric(year),
    country_join = recode(country,
      "United States" = "United States of America",
      "Bolivia" = "Bolivia (Plurinational State of)",
      "Venezuela" = "Venezuela (Bolivarian Republic of)"
    ),
    country_key = clean_country(country_join)
  )

cases_year_clean <- cases_year |>
  mutate(
    year = as.numeric(year),
    country_key = clean_country(country)
  )

cases_year_gdp <- cases_year_clean |>
  left_join(
    gdp_web_clean |>
      select(country_key, year, gdp_per_capita),
    by = c("country_key", "year")
  )

# join the yearly gdp/cases data to the monthly cases data
cases_gdp <- cases_month |>
  left_join(
    cases_year_gdp,
    by = c("country", "year"),
    suffix = c("_month", "_year")
  ) |>
  # make a tibble to join
  as_tibble()
```

### Data Visualizations

#### Preparing data for visualization by removing year not available, renaming region acronyms for their representative names, and removing missing data.

```{r}
# create yearly country-level dataset with GDP joined
cases_gdp_year <- cases_year |>
  filter(year != 2025) |>
  mutate(
    year = as.numeric(year),
    measles_epi_linked = as.numeric(measles_epi_linked)
  ) |>
  left_join(
    gdp_web |>
      rename(year = scraped_year) |>
      select(country, year, gdp_per_capita),
    by = c("country", "year")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    region = recode(region,
      "AFRO" = "Africa",
      "AMRO" = "Americas",
      "EMRO" = "Eastern Mediterranean",
      "EURO" = "Europe",
      "SEARO" = "South-East Asia",
      "WPRO" = "Western Pacific"
    )
  ) |>
  filter(
    !is.na(measles_epi_linked),
    !is.na(gdp_per_capita),
    measles_epi_linked > 0
  )
```

#### GDP per capita Visual 1

```{r}
#| fig-cap-location: bottom
#| fig-cap: Figure 3. GDP per capita over years by region.
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 16
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-alt: "Faceted scatterplot of epidemiologically linked measles cases over time by region, with log-scaled y-axis and point color representing GDP per capita."

# faceted plot by region
ggplot(cases_gdp_year,
       aes(x = year,
           y = measles_epi_linked,
           color = gdp_per_capita)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.9, size = 3, width = 0.25, height = 0.1) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2012, 2016, 2020, 2024)) +
  scale_color_gradient(
    low = "#A6DAF5",
    high = "#3E3F3A",
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ region, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Epidemiologically Linked Measles Cases Over Time by Region",
    subtitle = "Each point represents a country-year; color shows GDP per capita",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Epidemiologically linked measles cases (log scale)",
    color = "GDP per capita"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 25)
```

#### GDP per capita Visual 2 and 3

```{r}
#| fig-cap-location: bottom
#| fig-cap: "Figure 4. Epidemiologically Linked Measles Cases and GDP per Capita by Region"
#| warning: false
#| fig-alt: "Two side-by-side ridgeline density plots by region. The left plot shows the distribution of epidemiologically linked measles cases across country-year observations from 2012 to 2024. The right plot shows the distribution of GDP per capita across the same regions and years."
#| fig-width: 18
#| fig-height: 8

epi_region_plot <- cases_gdp_year |>
  ggplot(aes(x = log1p(measles_epi_linked),
             y = region,
             fill = ..x..)) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 2, rel_min_height = 0.01) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = log1p(c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000)),
    labels = scales::comma(c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000))
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#F8F5F0",
    high = "#325D88"
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0.0001, 0.5)) +
  labs(
    #title = "Distribution of Epidemiologically Linked Measles Cases by Region",
    #subtitle = "Country-year observations from 2012–2024",
    x = "Epidemiologically linked measles cases (log scale)",
    y = "",
    fill = "Cases"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

gdp_region <- cases_gdp_year |>
  ggplot(aes(x = gdp_per_capita,
             y = region,
             fill = ..x..)) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 2, rel_min_height = 0.01) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#A6DAF5",
    high = "#3E3F3A"
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0.0001, 0.5)) +
  labs(
    #title = "Distribution of GDP per Capita by Region",
    #subtitle = "Country-year observations from 2012–2024",
    x = "GDP per capita (log scale)",
    y = "",
    fill = "GDP per capita"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

epi_region_plot + gdp_region +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Distributions of Measles Cases and GDP per Capita by Region",
    subtitle = "These plots show country-year distributions from 2012–2024",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 26),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 18)
    )
  )
```

### Table 1

```{r}
#| tbl-cap-location: bottom
#| tbl-cap: Table 3. Confirmed Measles Cases that are Epidemiologically-linked

# function to get proportion of cases that are epidemiologically-linked for each year in one region
epi_proportions <- function(region_code) {
  cases_month |>
    filter(.data$region == region_code) |>
    group_by(year) |>
    # get total counts for each year
    summarise(
      # measles total epidemiologically-linked and lab confirmed
      measles_epi_linked = sum(measles_epi_linked, na.rm = TRUE),
      measles_lab_confirmed = sum(measles_lab_confirmed, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    # modify columns by calculating proportions
    mutate(
      prop_epi_measles = measles_epi_linked / 
        (measles_epi_linked + measles_lab_confirmed)
    ) |>
    select(year, prop_epi_measles)
}

cases_month |>
  distinct(region) |>
  # rename regions to full names
  mutate(
    region_name = recode(region,
      "AFR" = "Africa",
      "AMR" = "Americas",
      "EMR" = "Eastern Mediterranean",
      "EUR" = "Europe",
      "SEAR" = "South-East Asia",
      "WPR" = "Western Pacific"
    )
  ) |>
  # apply function to get proportions for each region
  mutate(epi_props = map(region, epi_proportions)) |>
  # get columns from returned map lists
  unnest(epi_props) |>
  # keep only region name, year, and proportion
  select(region_name, year, prop_epi_measles) |>
  # group by region so the table is divided by region
  group_by(region_name) |>
  # make a gt table
  gt() |>
  # format the proportion columns to be percents 
  fmt_percent(columns = matches("prop"), decimals = 1) |>
  # bold region group labels
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_row_groups()
  ) |>
  # rename and bold column names
  cols_label(
    region_name = md("**Region**"),
    year = md("**Year**"),
    prop_epi_measles = md("**Epidemiologically-linked measles cases**")
  ) |>
  # add a table title
  tab_header(
    title = md("Proportion of Confirmed Cases that are Epidemiologically-linked")
  ) |>
  # add a table caption
  tab_caption(
    caption = md("Changes in each region's confirmed measles cases makeup over time.")
  )
```

### Table 2

```{r}
#| tbl-cap-location: bottom
#| tbl-cap: Table 4. Epidemiologically-linked counts by GDP levels of regions.

# calculation of global GDP level groups: low, medium, and high
cases_gdp_year_global <- cases_gdp_year |>
  mutate(
    gdp_group = ntile(gdp_per_capita, 3),
    gdp_group = recode(as.character(gdp_group),
      "1" = "Low GDP",
      "2" = "Medium GDP",
      "3" = "High GDP"
    ),
    gdp_group = factor(gdp_group,
      levels = c("Low GDP", "Medium GDP", "High GDP")
    )
  )

# function to get total epidemiologically-linked cases by GDP group for one region
gdp_epi_groups <- function(region_name) {
  cases_gdp_year_global |>
    filter(.data$region == region_name) |>
    group_by(gdp_group) |>
    summarise(
      epi_linked_cases = sum(measles_epi_linked, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

cases_gdp_year |>
  distinct(region) |>
  # apply function to get GDP groups for each region
  mutate(gdp_epi = map(region, gdp_epi_groups)) |>
  # get columns from returned map lists
  unnest(gdp_epi) |>
  # keep GDP levels with 0 cases
  complete(region, gdp_group, fill = list(epi_linked_cases = 0)) |>
  # group by region so the table is divided by region
  group_by(region) |>
  # make a gt table
  gt() |>
  # format the epidemiologically-linked case counts
  fmt_number(columns = epi_linked_cases, decimals = 0) |>
  # bold region group labels
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_row_groups()
  ) |>
  # rename and bold column names
  cols_label(
    region = md("**Region**"),
    gdp_group = md("**GDP Level**"),
    epi_linked_cases = md("**Epidemiologically-linked measles cases**")
  ) |>
  # add a table title
  tab_header(
    title = md("Epidemiologically-linked Measles Cases by Regional GDP Level")
  ) |>
  # add a table caption
  tab_caption(
    caption = md("Total epidemiologically-linked measles cases grouped by global GDP per capita low, medium, and 
                 high within each region. Based on yearly country observations from 2012–2024.")
  )
```
